rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Appoint Katherine Utterback As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Pltf And For Leave To File 1st Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, January 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff's motion to appoint Katherine Utterback as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff has satisfied all procedural requirements under CCP §377.32 to establish Katherine Utterback as successor in interest for purposes of bring a survival action. Ms. Utterback signed a declaration pursuant to §377.32 under penalty of perjury which states that she is authorized to act as the successor-in-interest. The cases cited by Defendant Monterey Mechanical are distinguishable. All involved contests as to who was the rightful successor-in-interest amongst surviving heirs. Here, no heirs or beneficiaries have contested Ms. Utterback's appointment. Probate code §8468 strongly suggests that it is not necessary for the next of kin to be appointed as decedent's representative unless he or she claims a right of appointment. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL RABENER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, January 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. The deposition testimony of Leslie Wilkes creates a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to Bendix brakes. The objections to Walker's testimony are overruled. Honeywell's motion for summary adjudication is also denied. Honeywell has not shifted the burden of production with respect to any of the issues for which it seeks summary adjudication. Honeywell fails to separate each cause of action and claim of damages in its separate statement of facts and further fails to provide sufficient evidence to satisfy its burden as to each issue. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES GUTHRIE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, January 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Union Carbide's motion for summary judgment is denied. Union Carbide's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to exposure (issues 1-3) and moot as to false representation and punitive damages (issues 4-5) since plaintiff dismissed these claims against Union Carbide on 1/5/2010. Plaintiff presented sufficient evidence to permit a reasonable fact-finder to draw the inference that he was exposed to asbestos in "red dot" Hamilton joint compound attributable to Union Carbide. Union Carbide's objections to Guthrie's declaration and Hamilton's prior testimony are overruled. =(220/HEK)
